title: Why, My Heart, Do We Love Her So?
content:
Why, my heart, do we love her so?
    (Geraldine, Geraldine!)
    Why does the great sea ebb and flow? -
    Why does the round world spin?
    Geraldine, Geraldine,
    Bid me my life renew:
    What is it worth unless I win,
    Love - love and you?

    Why, my heart, when we speak her name
    (Geraldine, Geraldine!)
    Throbs the word like a flinging flame? -
    Why does the Spring begin?
    Geraldine, Geraldine,
    Bid me indeed to be:
    Open your heart, and take us in,
    Love - love and me.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Ernest Henley, titled "Why, My Heart, Do We Love Her So?", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
